exercises.name: Body Weight Calf Raise
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, keep your hips in line with your body as well as your knees straight. Hinge only at the ankles. 

2) Roll all the way up to your toes.

exercises.name: Body Weight Forward Lunge
exercises.description:
1) As you step forward, plant your foot, bend your knees so that you reach 90 degrees. 

2) Be careful that your front knee doesn’t lunge forward. Keep back tall over top of the back knee. 

3) Return to start position. 

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Body Weight Reverse Lunge
exercises.description:
1) As you step back, plant your foot and put your body weight over top of your back knee. 

2) Keep your body position tall. All lower body segments in 90 degrees. 

3) Return to start position. 

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Body Weight Single Leg Deadlift
exercises.alternateName: BW SL DL with knee lift
exercises.description:
1)  When performing this exercise, ensure that your upper body maintains a safe position for your back. Chest up, a slight arch or neutral spine.  

2) Stand on one leg and tip at the hips sitting back into your heel on your standing leg. 

3) Squeeze your glutes and stand. 

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Body Weight Squat
exercises.description:
1)  When performing the squat, let your body weight shift back into your heels as you sit your hips back. 

2) Keep your knees as far back behind your toes as possible. 

3) Keep your chest up and back held in neutral position or with a slight arch. 

4) Prevent yourself collapsing forward as you perform a squat. 

5) Make sure your toes, knees, and hips are all in line as you squat back.

exercises.name: Body Weight Step Up
exercises.description:
1. For this exercise, you’ll want to keep as much body weight as you can over top of the leg that’s gonna be doing most of the stepping up.

2. Place the right foot on the elevated platform. And let the left foot stay on the ground. This will be your starting position.

3. Lift your left foot all the way forward while keeping your knee slightly bent. And your right foot straight.

4. Then back to starting position.

5. Once you’re completed one side, do the other.

exercises.name: Body Weight Turkish Get Up
exercises.description:
1.Lie on the floor with your arm straight up over your chest and one leg bent at the hip and knee.

2.Thrust the bent leg forward raising your upper body off the floor, and coming up into a seated position then come up onto one knee with your arm straight.

3.Stand upright with your arm fully extended overhead.

4.Reverse the steps to lower back down to the start position. Complete all reps on one side before switching to the other side.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Alternating Cossack Squat
exercises.description:
Perform a side-to-side squat, alternating between each leg. Lower your body toward one leg, keeping the other leg straight, then return to the starting position and switch sides. 

This enhances hip mobility, strength, and flexibility.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Alternating Step Up
exercises.description:
Perform a step up, alternating legs with each repetition. 

This variation increases coordination and balance, engaging your quads, glutes, and calves.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Arms Overhead Squat
exercises.description: Perform a squat while keeping both arms extended overhead, ensuring a neutral spine and deep squat depth.

exercises.name: Bodyweight A-skip
exercises.description: Perform a skipping motion, alternating knees up toward the chest while driving the opposite arm forward.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Bear Squat To Plank
exercises.description:
Start in bear position (on hands and knees, hips lifted). Squat back by lowering your hips and sitting into a squat, then transition into a plank position by straightening your legs and bringing your body into a straight line. 

Targets core, shoulders, and legs.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Bent Knee Calf Raise
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet shoulder-width apart and knees slightly bent. Lift your heels off the ground by pushing through the balls of your feet, engaging your calves. Slowly lower back down. 

Strengthens calves and ankles.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Bent Knee Single Leg Calf Raise
exercises.description:
Stand on one leg with your knee slightly bent. Raise your heel off the ground by pushing through the ball of your foot.

Lower slowly. Focuses on calf strength, balance, and stability.

exercises.name: Bodyweight B-skip
exercises.description:
Perform a skipping movement by lifting one knee to waist height, then extending your leg straight out in front of you while hopping on the other foot. 

Alternate legs with each skip. This improves hip flexor and hamstring mobility.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Cossack Squat
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and your toes pointed outward. Shift your weight to one leg, bending the knee while keeping the other leg straight. 

Lower your hips as far as possible while maintaining good posture, then return to standing. Targets legs, hips, and improves flexibility.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Crab Reach
exercises.description:
Start seated with your hands behind you, knees bent, and feet flat on the floor.

Lift your hips off the ground and rotate one arm overhead while keeping your hips elevated. This engages your chest, shoulders, and core.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Deadbug
exercises.description:
Lie on your back with arms extended towards the ceiling and knees bent. 

Lower opposite arm and leg towards the floor, then return to start. Alternate sides.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Deadlift
exercises.description: Perform a hinge movement by pushing your hips back, lowering your torso while maintaining a flat back, then standing up again.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Kang Squat
exercises.description:
Start standing, hinge forward at the hips into a good-morning position, then drop into a squat. 

Reverse the movement to return to standing.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Lateral Squat
exercises.description: Step laterally into a squat position, bending one knee while keeping the other leg straight, and return to center.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Rear Foot Elevated Squat
exercises.description:
Place one foot on a bench or elevated surface behind you. Lower your hips and bend the front knee to perform a squat, keeping your chest lifted. 

This targets quads, glutes, and improves balance.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Reverse Plank
exercises.description: Sit with legs extended, then lift your hips off the ground into a reverse plank position, holding for time while engaging the core.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Single Leg Calf Raise
exercises.description:
Stand on one leg and slowly raise your heel off the ground. Focus on pushing through the ball of your foot. 

Strengthens calves and improves balance.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Single Side Step Up
exercises.description:
Stand in front of a bench or sturdy platform. 

Step one foot onto the platform and drive through your heel to lift your body up, bringing the opposite knee towards your chest. 

Step back down and alternate legs for a functional lower body workout.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Snap Down
exercises.description: From a standing position, rise up onto your toes with arms overhead, then quickly drop into an athletic stance by bending your knees and swinging your arms back, landing softly. Reset and repeat the snapping motion continuously.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Spiderman Lunge To Rotation
exercises.description:
Start in a plank position. Bring one foot toward your hand in a lunge position, then rotate your torso to reach your opposite arm towards the sky. 

This engages the core, shoulders, and hips.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Split Squat
exercises.description:
1)  While performing this exercise, ensure that your body is over top of your back leg. 

2) Keep all your lower body segments at 90 degrees. 

3) Extend up into a standing position. 

4) Repeat.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Squat To Hinge
exercises.alternateName: Squat to stand, Deep Squat Hinge
exercises.description:
Start in a deep squat position, then straighten your legs and shift your hips back into a hinge position. 

Engage your hamstrings and lower back. Focuses on leg mobility and core stability.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Sumo Squat
exercises.description:
Stand with your feet wider than shoulder-width apart and toes pointed outward. 

Lower into a squat, keeping your knees in line with your toes. 

This targets the quads, glutes, and inner thighs.

exercises.name: Bodyweight Walking Lunge
exercises.description:
Perform a lunge while walking forward, alternating legs with each step. 

Focus on a deep lunge and proper posture.

exercises.name: BOSU Alternating Lateral Squat Shuffle
exercises.alternateName: Bosu Right Side Up Side Squat Hop Plyometric Lateral Jump
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, ensure that you try to land directly on top of the BOSU. You’ll also want to land in a nice squat form. 

2) Place one foot in the top center of the dome of the BOSU. The other foot should be placed wider than shoulder width apart on the ground beside the BOSU. 

3) Begin getting into a squat: hinge at the hip, keep a neutral or slightly extended spine, and bend at the knees.

4) Simultaneously shift your body weight over the BOSU as you lean to the middle, and jump off both feet. 

5) Land with the foot from the ground landing on the top of the BOSU, and the foot originally on the BOSU landing on the other side of the BOSU wider than shoulder width apart. 

6) Repeat until your prescribed reps.

exercises.name: BOSU Burpee
exercises.description:
Hold a BOSU ball dome-side down on the floor from the start. Get into a plank position with your hands gripping the sides of the BOSU. Perform a push-up, then jump your feet forward toward your hands. 

Explosively jump upward while pressing the BOSU fully overhead with extended arms. 

Land softly back into the plank position with hands on the BOSU and repeat continuously, keeping your core engaged throughout.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Down Dumbbell Squat and Press
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure your core is engaged the whole time to help prevent wobbling on the BOSU.

2) As you step up on the BOSU, try to have your feet roughly the same distance from the mid line on either side.

3) When you squat, try to maintain proper squatting form which means your knees are in line with your second toe and your hips

4) As you press up overhead, have your biceps come in line with your ears beside your head.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Down Dumbbell Thruster
exercises.description:
1.When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure your core is engaged. Also, you’ll want to execute a proper squat which means your knees will track in line with your second toe and your hips.

2.Step one foot at a time onto the flat side of the BOSU with your feet placed equal distances apart. Lift the dumbbells to your shoulder height. 

3.Lower yourself into a squat. In one fluid movement both stand up from your squat and press the dumbbells overhead fully with your arms touching your ears and arms straight. 

4.Then, in one movement, bring the weights back to your shoulders while squatting again.

5.Repeat prescribed number of repetitions.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Down Elbow Plank
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure your abdominals and glutes are active. This will help you maintain neutral spine throughout.

2) You’ll want to place your elbows at equal distance from the center line of the BOSU ball.

3) Engage your abdominals and glutes. Slowly extend your legs out behind you one at a time. 

4) Raise your body to make a straight line along your shoulders, neutral spine, hips, knees, and ankles. Ensure your shoulders are directly above your elbows.

5) Hold for the prescribed duration of time, or until your form is lost.

exercises.name: BOSU Dome Down Full Plank
exercises.alternateName: high plank, straight arm plank bosu
exercises.description:
1) When performing this exercise, you’ll want to ensure your hands are placed equal distance away from one another.

2) You’ll also want to keep your abdominals engaged and glutes on to maintain neutral spine throughout.

3) Raise your body to make a straight line along your shoulders, neutral spine, hips, knees, and feet. Ensure your shoulders are directly above your hands.

4) Hold for the prescribed duration of time, or until your form is lost
